entry.value: import knime.scripting.io as knio%%00010import pandas as pd%%00010import requests%%00010import json%%00010%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# 0. LOAD SECRETS FROM CSV READER (BOTTOM PORT)%%00010# ==========================================%%00010df_env = knio.input_tables[1].to_pandas()%%00010env_dict = dict(zip(df_env.iloc[:, 0].str.strip(), df_env.iloc[:, 1].str.strip()))%%00010%%00010API_KEY = env_dict.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")%%00010url = env_dict.get("OPENROUTER_URL")%%00010%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# 1. READ TRANSPOSED VERTICAL HEADERS%%00010# ==========================================%%00010df_input = knio.input_tables[0].to_pandas()%%00010messy_headers = df_input['Column Header'].dropna().unique().tolist()%%00010%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# 2. SETUP OPENROUTER AI%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# Your EXACT 13 Golden Standard Targets from the screenshot%%00010target_columns = [%%00010 "Date", %%00010 "Importer Name", %%00010 "Importer Country", %%00010 "Exporter Name", %%00010 "Exporter Country", %%00010 "HS Code", %%00010 "Product Description", %%00010 "Product Category", %%00010 "Product", %%00010 "Quantity", %%00010 "Quantity Unit", %%00010 "Total Price (USD)", %%00010 "Unit Price (USD/QTY)"%%00010]%%00010%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# 3. THE STRICT AI PROMPT%%00010# ==========================================%%00010system_prompt = f"""%%00010You are an expert enterprise data engineer. I will provide a list of messy column headers from a global trade dataset. %%00010Your job is to map them to EXACTLY these 13 Golden Standard targets:%%00010{target_columns}%%00010%%00010CRITICAL REASONING RULES:%%000101. THE (EN) PRIORITY: If multiple messy headers mean the same thing (e.g., 'Product' and 'Product(EN)'), you MUST select the exact header containing '(EN)' as the absolute winner.%%000102. PRODUCT DISTINCTIONS: %%00010 - 'Product' = Base product name.%%00010 - 'Product Description' = Detailed description or item details.%%00010 - 'Product Category' = The type, group, or classification of the product.%%00010 Do not merge them. If multiple exist, map them to their respective targets.%%000103. CONTEXTUAL MEANING: Analyze the business context. 'Destination Country' means 'Importer Country'. 'Origin' means 'Exporter Country'.%%000104. THE ASIAN TAG RULE: NEVER select headers containing (VN), (CN), (ID), (TH), or (KR). Ignore them completely.%%000105. THE DATE RULE: NEVER select dates containing 'registration', 'departure', 'arrival', 'release', or 'estimated'.%%000106. STRICT MATCHING: Only choose headers that actually exist in the user's provided list. If no header logically matches a target, map it to null.%%00010%%00010OUTPUT FORMAT:%%00010Return ONLY a valid JSON object where the KEY is the Golden Standard Target (from my list), and the VALUE is the exact Messy Header you selected.%%00010"""%%00010%%00010headers = {%%00010 "Authorization": f"Bearer {API_KEY}",%%00010 "HTTP-Referer": "https://my-knime-pipeline.com", %%00010 "X-Title": "KNIME Dictionary Builder", %%00010 "Content-Type": "application/json"%%00010}%%00010%%00010payload = {%%00010 "model": "anthropic/claude-3-haiku", %%00010 "messages": [%%00010 {"role": "system", "content": system_prompt},%%00010 {"role": "user", "content": json.dumps(messy_headers)}%%00010 ],%%00010 "response_format": { "type": "json_object" }, %%00010 "temperature": 0.0 %%00010}%%00010%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# 4. EXECUTE AI MAPPING%%00010# ==========================================%%00010try:%%00010 response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload, timeout=45)%%00010 response.raise_for_status() %%00010 result = response.json()%%00010 %%00010 target_to_messy = json.loads(result['choices'][0]['message']['content'])%%00010 %%00010except Exception as e:%%00010 print(f"API Failed: {e}")%%00010 target_to_messy = {}%%00010%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# 5. FORMAT FOR KNIME'S DICTIONARY RENAMER%%00010# ==========================================%%00010mapping_data = []%%00010%%00010for golden_target, messy_origin in target_to_messy.items():%%00010 if messy_origin and str(messy_origin).strip().lower() != "null": %%00010 mapping_data.append({%%00010 "Old_Name": messy_origin,%%00010 "New_Name": golden_target%%00010 })%%00010%%00010dict_df = pd.DataFrame(mapping_data)%%00010%%00010# Safety Net: Ensure output table structure exists even if AI fails%%00010if dict_df.empty:%%00010 dict_df = pd.DataFrame(columns=["Old_Name", "New_Name"])%%00010%%00010# ==========================================%%00010# 6. SEND DICTIONARY BACK TO KNIME%%00010# ==========================================%%00010knio.output_tables[0] = knio.Table.from_pandas(dict_df)
